title: inula × hispida
intro: Inula × hispida, also known as the hairy inulin or golden ragwort, is a hybrid plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Inula × hispida include reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. Inula × hispida is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
